topic: Senate Blue Ribbon Committee expanding flood control probe while impeachment trial budget constraints dominate headlines
tweet1: Sen. Erwin Tulfo says the Blue Ribbon Committee will expand its flood control probe to include Taguig City — days after the Senate said it has no budget to feed lawyers during the Duterte impeachment trial.
tweet2: Per @gmanews: Tulfo confirmed Lacson will submit documents on Taguig flood control issues. Senate already investigating multiple flood control anomalies worth billions. But leadership claims walang pondo for trial catering.
tweet3: The Senate's investigative machinery keeps running — subpoenas, hearings, witnesses — while the same body says feeding defense lawyers during a constitutional trial is beyond its fiscal capacity. Which expenditure is actually discretionary?
tweet4: The budget exists. The question is priority: constitutional duty vs. legislative oversight. Senate already chose. Has anyone pulled the Blue Ribbon Committee's operational budget vs. the catering cost they're refusing?
